New Philosophies for Graduate Education: A Conversation with Barry Lam *07
snippet.description:
How might we imagine the tenure track in ways that account both for individual preference and for societal impact? In what ways is the landscape for Ph.D.s changing, and how might graduate students best navigate this changing landscape? 

 Join Barry Lam *07 and Ph.D. student Hellen Wainaina for an engaging and personal conversation about the ways they are rethinking what a Ph.D. can be. You'll be encouraged to center your own strengths, values, and preferences, and you'll hear firsthand how academia's relationship to publicly-engaged scholarship is changing--and why it must!

 Led by Princeton graduate student Hellen Wainaina, the GradFUTURES podcast centers on the futures of PhDs: both those in training at Princeton, and Princeton graduate alumni who are in and beyond academia. The podcast tells the professional development stories of graduate students, graduate alumni, and those who partner and collaborate with them.

snippet.title: Quanta Innovation for Smarter Health
snippet.description:
Through a longstanding collaboration with MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), Quanta Computer, the world’s largest computer maker, has contributed to the AIoT innovation and helped introduce the AIoT concept for connected health. It has also paved the way for the advancement of AI Medicine, utilizing advanced machine learning algorithms to analyze vast amounts of clinical and patient data, ultimately generating AI models and Digital Twins for enabling prediction and precision health. The culmination of these efforts has materialized into the creation of the QOCA (Quanta Omni-Care with AI) solutions, empowering healthcare providers to accelerate their journey of digital health transformation.

Interview Credits (in the order of appearance):
Dr. Barry Lam, Chairman of Quanta Computer
Dr. Ted Chang, CTO of Quanta Computer
Prof. Huey-Jen Jenny Su, Former President of National Cheng-Kung University
Prof. John Guttag, Lead of Clinical and Applied Machine Group, MIT
Prof. Collin Stultz, Director of Harvard-MIT Health Sciences & Technology
Prof. Regina Barzilay, AI Faculty Lead, Jameel Clinic, MIT
Prof. Victor Sue, Former CSAIL Director, MIT

Special thanks to Taichung Veterans General Hospital for filming location support.

@QuantaResearch @mit @MITCSAIL  #AI #SmartHealthcare #DigitalHealth #AIoT #MedicalAI #AIMedicine #SmartMedicine #QuantaComputer #QuantaResearch #QOCA

snippet.title: INILAH 5 CIRI PEMIMPIN UNGGUL | Riset & Buku James Kouzes & Barry Posner di LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE
snippet.description:
INILAH 5 CIRI PEMIMPIN UNGGUL | Riset & Buku James Kouzes & Barry Posner di LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE

Kali ini, yuk kita simak 5 ciri pemimpin unggul menurut riset dua akademisi dan pembicara manajemen James Kouzes & Barry Posner. Riset itu ditulis di buku mereka di tahun 1987 yakni THE LEADERSHIP CHALLENGE. Meskipun sudah hampir 35 tahun lebih. Faktanya, apa yg dikatakan oleh kedua profesor ini masih tetap relevan. Bukunya masih dicetak dan masih tetap menginspirasi. Nah, apakah ke 5 karakter itu? Simak penjelasannya di video ini. Pada prinsipnya ada 5 karakter utama pemimpin unggul ini:
1. Menginspirasi visi bersama (Inspire Shared Vision)
2. Menantang proses yang ada (Challenge the Process)
3. Mendorong hati orang (Encouraging the Heart)
4. Memampukan orang lain untuk bertindak (Enabling others to act)
5. Memberikan keteladanan dan jadi role model (Model the way)

Buat para leaders dan pemimpin, doaku semoga Anda bisa jadi pemimpin dengan karakter utama seperti itu, yang membuat Anda selalu dikenang dengan manis!

snippet.title: Ann Beattie — Onlookers
snippet.description:
Watch author Ann Beattie's book talk and reading at Politics and Prose book store in Washington, D.C.

PURCHASE BOOK HERE: https://www.politics-prose.com/book/9781668013656

Onlookers is an astute new story collection about people living in the same Southern town whose lives intersect in surprising ways. Peaceful Charlottesville, Virginia, drew national attention when white nationalists held a rally there in 2017, a horrific event whose repercussions are still felt today. Confederate monuments such as General Robert E. Lee atop his horse were then still standing. The statues are a constant presence and a metaphoric refrain throughout this collection, though they represent different things to different characters. Some landmarks may have faded from consciousness but provoke fresh outrage when viewed through newly opened eyes.

In "Nearby," an elderly man and his younger wife watch from their penthouse as protestors gather to oppose the once "heroic" explorers Lewis and Clark depicted towering over their native guide, Sacagawea. A lawyer in "In the Great Southern Tradition" deals with a crisis on Richmond's Monument Avenue, while his sister and nephew plant tulip bulbs at her stately home.

These are stories of unexpected relationships and affiliations that affirm the value of friendship, even when it requires difficult compromises or unexpected risks. Beattie involves the reader in questions about the nature of community, as the characters grapple with complicated inheritances that are both historical and personal and the realities of their lives interact uneasily with the past.

Ann Beattie has been included in five O. Henry Award Collections, in The Best American Short Stories and The Best American Short Stories of the Century. She is the recipient of the PEN/Malamud Award for achievement in the short story. In 2005, she received the Rea Award for the Short Story. The former Edgar Allan Poe Professor of Literature and Creative Writing at the University of Virginia, she is a member of The American Academy of Arts and Letters and of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. She and her husband, Lincoln Perry, live in Maine, Virginia, and Florida.

snippet.title: New* Li'l Red Riding Hood - Sam the Sham & the Pharaohs (no cartoons HD and in stereo)
snippet.description:
Jukebox Alive, with the help of a mighty Wurlitzer jukebox, presents a new and improved video of Sam the Sham & the Pharaohs singing their 1966 smash hit Li'l Red Riding Hood. Unlike other online versions of Sam singing this song, there is no Betty Boop or any other cartoon filler. It is all Sam, his band and the '60s go-go girls. This video was created from the best DVD source material I could locate. For the record, this performance was from the Milton Berle show on Sept. 23, 1966. That night the band performed a medley of "Li'l Red Riding Hood" and his then more recent hit "Hair On My Chinny Chin Chin." Prior to this video, since the song had been abbreviated at the time, editors had filled in the gaps with cartoon characters.

Personal Notes:
I remember Li'l Red Riding Hood" as a giant hit from the summer of 1966. There are only a few videos of Sam the Sham performing this one, so I am posting it for historical purposes. Unlike other online versions of Sam singing this song, there is no Betty Boop or any other cartoon filler. It is all Sam, his band and the '60s go-go girls. snippet.title: Sebastian Barry in conversation with Professor Roy Foster
snippet.description:
Recording of Sebastian Barry in conversation with Professor Roy Foster during the 2018 Liverpool Literary Festival, hosted by the University of Liverpool and sponsored by the Institute of Irish Studies.

Author and playwright Sebastian Barry was born in Dublin. His novels and plays have won the Costa Book of the Year Award, the Kerry Group Irish Fiction Prize, the Irish Book Awards Novel of the Year, the Independent Booksellers Prize and the James Tait Black Memorial Prize. He also had two consecutive novels, A Long Long Way (2005) and the top ten bestseller The Secret Scripture (2008), shortlisted for the MAN Booker Prize. Days Without End (2016) won the Costa Book Award and the Walter Scott Prize for Historical Fiction.

Professor Roy Foster is Emeritus Professor of Irish History at Oxford and a renowned critic and broadcaster. A Fellow of the British Academy, he has received numerous honorary degrees and university fellowships. His publications cover many topics about Irish history, and figures such as Charles Stewart Parnell, Lord Randolph Churchill and W.B. Yeats. He won the 1998 James Tait Black Prize for biography and the 2016 Frokosch Prize from the American Historical Association.

snippet.title: The Latest Science of Growth Mindset || Carol Dweck
snippet.description:
This episode is part of The Psychology Podcast's "Best of Series", where we highlight some of the most exciting, enthralling, and enlightening episodes from our archives.

Today it’s a real honor to have Carol Dweck on the podcast. Dr. Dweck is a leading researcher in the field of motivation and is the Lewis and Virginia Eaton Professor of Psychology at Stanford. Her research examines the role of mindsets in personal achievement and organizational effectiveness. 

Dr. Dweck has also held professorships at Columbia and Harvard Universities, has lectured to education, business, and sports groups around the world, has addressed the United Nations, has been elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences and the National Academy of Sciences, and has won 12 lifetime achievement awards for her research. Her best-selling book Mindset has been widely influential and has been translated into over 25 languages. 

Website: https://profiles.stanford.edu/carol-dweck


Topics

01:40 Incremental vs. entity beliefs

06:22 Fixed vs. growth mindset

11:01 Mindset interventions

18:47 National Study of Learning Mindsets

32:33 The role of peers and teachers 

38:44 Growth mindset and other outcomes in life

45:51 Giftedness exists

48:38 Praising effort and intelligence

50:45 How a fixed mindset can sometimes lead to increased performance

52:50 Cross-cultural differences in mindsets

57:19 Criticisms of the growth mindset

1:03:14 “Mindset is not a miracle maker” 

1:06:24 Further studies on the growth mindset
